puzzle.title: The March of Futility
puzzle.intro: A mighty army, confident in its power and led by a colossal beast, set out with a singular, destructive purpose. They believed their sheer force could desecrate a sacred sanctuary, but they were about to encounter a power far greater than their own. Can you uncover the identity of this arrogant legion and the divine intervention that thwarted their plan?
clues: We were a great multitude, traveling with a creature of immense size, certain of our victory.
Our leader, driven by envy, sought to divert the world's reverence from a sacred house to one he had built.
As we approached our target, our largest animal refused to advance, kneeling in submission to a will greater than our own.
We did not face a human army; instead, our doom came from the sky, carried by the smallest of messengers.
Swarms of birds appeared above us, each carrying stones that would seal our fate.
Our army was left like 'eaten straw,' a lesson for all who would challenge the sanctity of the Kaaba in the year of the Prophet's birth.

trial.label: What Was Our Mission?
options: To flood the city of Saba'
To build a tower to the heavens

location.label: What Was Sent Against Us?
options: A shower of fire and brimstone
A swarm of locusts and lice

outcome.label: What Was Our Fate?
options: Annihilated by stones of baked clay
Forced to retreat in a storm
outcome.answer: Annihilated by stones of baked clay

puzzle.verse: And He made them like eaten straw. (105:5)
